    "Isolated metal parts present on electrical equipment, creating a shock and arcing hazard",
    "Metal parts that are required to be bonded are not effectively bonded to the equipment grounding conductor",
    "Potential for energized metal parts to reach line-to-ground voltage with no low-impedance fault current path",
    "Condition violates NEC bonding requirements for separately derived systems and metal equipment"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately and de-energize the affected equipment if safe to do so",
    "Verify the bonding jumper connections between all normally non-current-carrying metal parts and the equipment grounding conductor",
    "Install or repair bonding jumpers per NEC 250.104 and 250.30",
    "Confirm continuity with a low-resistance ohmmeter and retest before re-energizing",
    "Tag and lock out the circuit until bonding is corrected and inspected"
  ],
  "rationale": "Isolated metal parts on operating electrical equipment defeat the bonding and grounding path required by NEC 250.104 and 250.30. This creates a high-severity shock and arcing hazard because fault current cannot reliably return to the source, and exposed metal can become energized. The installation is non-compliant and must be corrected before the asset is returned to service.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 250.104",
    "NEC 250.30"
  ]
}
